entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop used as a host for the shoot fly Atherigona soccata

entities.name: ATHERIGONA SOCCATA
entities.description: A shoot fly pest that oviposits on sorghum seedlings

entities.name: OVIPOSITION
entities.description: The process of laying eggs by the female shoot fly on host plants

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum genotypes like IS 18551 to resist shoot fly infestation

entities.name: SUSCEPTIBILITY
entities.description: The vulnerability of sorghum genotypes like CSH 5 to shoot fly infestation

entities.name: VOLATILES
entities.description: Chemical compounds emitted by sorghum seedlings that influence pest behavior

entities.name: GREENHOUSE CONDITIONS
entities.description: The controlled environment where the oviposition experiments were conducted

entities.name: SEEDLING AGE
entities.description: The age of sorghum seedlings, categorized as younger (5-10 DAE) or older (14-21 DAE)

entities.name: HOST PLANT ODOUR
entities.description: The scent emitted by the sorghum plant that attracts the pest

entities.name: FISHMEAL
entities.description: A substance used in treatments to compare its effect on pest response against host plant odour

entities.name: TRANSPARENT TRAP
entities.description: A type of trap used to attract and capture adult A. soccata

entities.name: LIVE FLY TRAP
entities.description: A type of trap used for comparison in adult attraction experiments

entities.name: BLACK TRAP
entities.description: A type of trap used for comparison in adult attraction experiments

entities.name: ADULT ATTRACTION
entities.description: The process by which adult A. soccata are drawn to specific stimuli

entities.name: PHOTOTACTIC STIMULI
entities.description: Optical stimuli that facilitate the orientation of the pest to its host
